evals.prompt: I'm building a small recipe-box app for home cooks (working name 'Ladle'). Before I write any code I want to see how it could look — can you design the main screens? Something warm and unfussy, phone app, light and dark mode. I want: home with saved recipes, a recipe detail, and what a brand-new user sees.
evals.expected_output: A mockup directory scaffolded from the skill assets with a customized design-system.css (warm palette, both themes), a filled DESIGN-SYSTEM.md contract, and an index.html whose sections cover home, detail, and first-run empty state with real copy, column labels, and rationale notes. Contrast pairs updated; foundations render green.

evals.prompt: We have a mockup directory at design/mockup built with the mobile-mockups-skill method (design-system.css, DESIGN-SYSTEM.md, index.html). Add a settings flow: notifications preferences, account, and a delete-account confirmation. Match the existing system exactly.
evals.expected_output: A new flow page (copied from flow.html, foundations: false) or new section using ONLY existing design-system values and .t-* roles — no new hexes or font sizes. Delete-account screen shows a danger treatment consistent with the contract; states beyond the happy path included; existing files not normalized or restyled.

evals.prompt: Our mockup's design system feels too corporate — client says it should feel 'like a cozy bookshop'. Can you rework the look without redoing every screen?
evals.expected_output: Changes concentrated in design-system.css role values (warmer surfaces, softer ink, serif display family, larger radii) plus DESIGN-SYSTEM.md updated to match; screens untouched or minimally touched. Contrast audit re-checked after the change; before/after foundations screenshots offered.
